_notes: City Kit Roads. LANE-FIT: road-straight is a 1x1 unit two-lane tile (measured, XZ-centered, Y=0 base). Forgiving-arcade target = 12 m two-lane tile (~6 m/lane vs the 1.8 m car), so scale = 12.0 (an initial 8 drove too tight). tile-high is 0.25 -> 0.25*12 = 3 m vertical cell, so cell_size [12,3,12]; paint the deck at y-index 1. GridMaps using this palette need cell_center_y = false. NB: road-curve is a 2x2-unit sweeping curve; road-bend is the tight 1x1 corner. Signs/lights/construction/bridge-pillars are props -> prefabs (the palette is for road/tile pieces only).

ports._notes: RoadPath endpoint sockets (docs/plans/road_sockets.md), consumed by kit/helpers/road_ports.gd. Measured from roads.meshlib: tiles are XZ-centered on the anchor cell, asphalt surface y=0.12 above the cell base (curb top 0.24), paved strip 9.6 m wide (road-straight runs along local X, asphalt z +/-4.8). `cell` is the anchor-relative offset of the port's home cell in the item's local frame (pre-rotation); `face` is the open face the road crosses. First matching entry wins (families-style regexes); tiles matching nothing have no ports. Deliberately portless: road-curve (2x2) and road-split (1x2) are centered pieces whose road ends land on half-cell planes (off-lattice, cannot mate with straights on this lattice); road-side* overhangs to z=-9.72; road-square's asphalt is a plaza that reaches no face; road-straight-half / -barrier-end / -barrier-half, slants, bridge, driveways are out of scope. road-roundabout is a lattice-aligned 3x3 (spans +/-18) with 4 exits on its outer ring.
